event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: One accepted LSLC-002F envelope is consumed into its uninterpreted query identifier and existing LSLC-002B typed observation without reparsing or alternate admission policy.
Boundary identifiers including zero and u64 maximum survive unchanged beside typed fields without equality, lookup, matching, correlation, freshness, or endpoint inference.
LSLC-002B admission failures are returned unchanged under one stage-typed response-observation error.

No correlation semantics, sockets, discovery runtime, endpoints, clocks/currentness, interoperability, activation, devices, dependencies, unsafe/FFI, private artifacts, or Manifold authority are added.

document.commit_policy: Commit the inert accepted-envelope-to-typed-observation composition separately from query correlation, discovery, networking, runtime activation, provider evidence, and authority.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the explicit response-envelope to typed-observation composition and preserve identifier non-semantics.
Describe the dependency-completing typed response observation without correlation or runtime claims.
Review public/private and Rusty LSL/Manifold routing; no shared-skill mutation required.
Review artifact composition and explicit authority closure; no shared-skill mutation required.

non_scope: Query-response correlation, equality or lookup policy, sockets, UDP, multicast, discovery runtime, endpoint ownership or selection, retries, clocks, provider/currentness evidence, or ecosystem interoperability.
Dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, devices, runtime activation, private artifacts, copied implementation source, or external oracle execution.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, session, topology, revision, replay, authorization, audit, transport, command, product, or provider authority.
document.objective: Consume one accepted LSLC-002F short-info response envelope into its unchanged uninterpreted query identifier and the existing LSLC-002B typed observed fields, with unchanged delegated admission errors and no correlation, runtime, endpoint, currentness, or authority semantics.
outputs: Typed response observation retaining the uninterpreted identifier beside existing typed fields.
Boundary and delegated-error tests, focused gate, Standard receipt, and executed no-force feature-push receipt.
